candidates.comparison: async TP compiled (VLLM_COMPILE with fuse_gemm_comms=True) vs standard TP (non-compiled, MP backend)
candidates.c1_weak_oracle: agree — exact dict equality via compare_two_settings -> compare_all_settings (line 1342: assert ref_result == compare_result) is a weak oracle on generated text/tokens
candidates.c2_realistic_breakage: agree — async TP compilation fuses GEMM+comm ops into different kernels than unfused TP; different numeric paths under PyTorch/cuBLAS changes can realistically alter floating-point results
candidates.c3_no_strong_contract: agree — Not Strong #2: non-compiled vs compiled mode parity inside compare_two_settings. Not Strong #5: async TP fused distributed compile-pass parity against unfused baseline. No contract requires these to produce identical output.
candidates.code_snippet:
compare_two_settings(
    model_id,
    async_tp_args,
    tp_args,
    method="generate",
    force_v1_runner=True,
)

candidates.comparison: async TP + SP compiled (VLLM_COMPILE with fuse_gemm_comms=True, enable_sp=True) vs standard TP (non-compiled, MP backend) for NVFP4 model
candidates.c1_weak_oracle: agree — exact dict equality via compare_two_settings -> compare_all_settings (line 1342: assert ref_result == compare_result) is a weak oracle on generated text/tokens
candidates.c2_realistic_breakage: agree — SP + async TP compile pass fuses GEMM+comm and enables sequence parallelism with different kernel dispatch and numeric paths than unfused TP
candidates.c3_no_strong_contract: agree — Not Strong #5: sequence parallel + async TP fused distributed compile-pass parity against an unfused baseline. No contract requires compiled+fused to match non-compiled.
candidates.code_snippet:
compare_two_settings(
    NVFP4_MODEL_ID,
    async_tp_args,
    tp_args,
    method="generate",
    force_v1_runner=True,
)

candidates.comparison: TP with SP compilation (VLLM_COMPILE, enable_sp=True) vs standard TP (non-compiled, MP backend)
candidates.c1_weak_oracle: agree — exact dict equality via _compare_sp -> compare_two_settings -> compare_all_settings (line 1342) is a weak oracle on generated text/tokens
candidates.c2_realistic_breakage: agree — SP compile pass changes tensor partitioning and reduction order, producing different floating-point accumulation than non-SP TP
candidates.c3_no_strong_contract: agree — Not Strong #5: sequence parallel compile-pass parity against unfused baseline. Not Strong #2: non-compiled vs compiled mode parity inside compare_two_settings. No contract requires these to match.
candidates.code_snippet:
_compare_sp(
    model_id,
    parallel_setup,
    distributed_backend,
    runner,
    test_options,
    num_gpus_available,
    use_inductor_graph_partition,
    fuse_gemm_comms=fuse_gemm_comms,
    enable_prompt_embeds=False,
    method="generate",
    is_multimodal=False,
)

candidates.comparison: TP with SP compilation (VLLM_COMPILE, enable_sp=True, prompt_embeds enabled) vs standard TP (non-compiled, prompt_embeds enabled)
candidates.c1_weak_oracle: agree — exact dict equality via _compare_sp -> compare_two_settings -> compare_all_settings (line 1342) is a weak oracle on generated text/tokens
candidates.c2_realistic_breakage: agree — SP compile pass with prompt_embeds graph-rewrite path changes tensor partitioning and reduction order; both sides use prompt_embeds so the divergence axis is compiled-SP vs non-compiled TP
candidates.c3_no_strong_contract: agree — Not Strong #5: sequence parallel compile-pass parity against unfused baseline. Both sides use prompt_embeds but the core comparison remains compiled vs non-compiled. No contract requires these to match.
candidates.code_snippet:
_compare_sp(
    "hmellor/tiny-random-LlamaForCausalLM",
    parallel_setup,
    distributed_backend="mp",
    runner="auto",
    test_options=SPTestOptions(multi_node_only=False, load_format=None),
    num_gpus_available=num_gpus_available,
    use_inductor_graph_partition=use_inductor_graph_partition,
    fuse_gemm_comms=False,
    enable_prompt_embeds=True,
    method="generate",
    is_multimodal=False,
)

candidates.comparison: TP with SP compilation (VLLM_COMPILE, enable_sp=True, fuse_norm_quant=True, fuse_act_quant=True) vs standard TP (non-compiled) for NVFP4 model
candidates.c1_weak_oracle: agree — exact dict equality via _compare_sp -> compare_two_settings -> compare_all_settings (line 1342) is a weak oracle on generated text/tokens
candidates.c2_realistic_breakage: agree — SP compile pass with fused norm/act quantization uses entirely different kernel chains than unfused TP; numeric paths diverge under PyTorch/cuBLAS changes
candidates.c3_no_strong_contract: agree — Not Strong #5: sequence parallel fused compile-pass parity against unfused baseline. Additional fusion (fuse_norm_quant, fuse_act_quant) further separates the compute paths. No contract requires these to match.
candidates.code_snippet:
_compare_sp(
    NVFP4_MODEL_ID,
    ParallelSetup(tp_size=2, pp_size=1, fuse_norm_quant=True,
        fuse_act_quant=True, eager_mode=True, chunked_prefill=False),
    "mp",
    "auto",
    SPTestOptions(multi_node_only=False, load_format="dummy",
        model_info=NVFP4_MODEL_INFO),
    num_gpus_available,
    use_inductor_graph_partition=False,
    fuse_gemm_comms=False,
    enable_prompt_embeds=False,
    method="generate",
    is_multimodal=False,
    dtype="bfloat16",
)
